FetchRequest,\n75\t        on_partial_result: Optional[Callable[[FetchSource, SourceResult], None]] = None,\n76\t    ) -> FetchResult:\n77\t        \"\"\"\n78\t        Execute fetch request with optional streaming callback.\n79\t\n80\t        Args:\n81\t            request: The fetch request to execute\n82\t            on_partial_result: Optional callback for streaming partial results\n83\t\n84\t        Returns:\n85\t            FetchResult with coverage information\n86\t        \"\"\"\n87\t        start_time = time.time()\n88\t\n89\t        # Get fetch commands for this intent type\n90\t        command_specs = get_fetch_commands(request.intent_type)\n91\t        required_sources = get_required_sources(request.intent_type)\n92\t\n93\t        logger.info(\n94\t            f\"Fetching {len(command_specs)} sources for intent {request.intent_id} \"\n95\t            f\"(type: {request.intent_type.value})\"\n96\t        )\n97\t\n98\t        # Create tasks for concurrent execution\n99\t        tasks = []\n100\t        for spec in command_specs:\n101\t            task = asyncio.create_task(\n102\t                self._execute_source(spec, request.context),\n103\t                name=f\"fetch_{spec.source.value}_{request.intent_id[:8]}\",\n104\t            )\n105\t            tasks.append((spec.source, spec.required, spec.timeout_seconds, task))\n106\t\n107\t        # Wait for all tasks to complete (with per-task timeout)\n108\t        sources = {}\n109\t        succeeded = []\n110\t        timed_out = []\n111\t        failed = []\n112\t        skipped = []\n113\t        caveats = []\n114\t\n115\t        for source, required, timeout, task in tasks:\n116\t            try:\n117\t                result = await asyncio.wait_for(task, timeout=timeout)\n118\t\n119\t                if on_partial_result:\n120\t                    # Stream partial result as it arrives\n121\t                    on_partial_result(source, result)\n122\t\n123\t                sources[source] = result\n124\t\n125\t                if result.status == \"success\":\n126\t                    succeeded.append(source)\n127\t                elif result.status == \"timeout\":\n128\t                    timed_out.append(source)\n129\t                    caveats.append(f\"{source.value} timed out\")\n130\t                elif result.status == \"error\":\n131\t                    failed.append(source)\n132\t                    if required:\n133\t                        caveats.append(f\"Required source {source.value} failed: {result.error}\")\n134\t                    else:\n135\t                        caveats.append(f\"Optional source {source.value} failed: {result.error}\")\n136\t\n137\t            except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n138\t                logger.warning(f\"Source {source.value} timed out after {timeout}s\")\n139\t                sources[source] = SourceResult(\n140\t                    source=source,\n141\t                    status=\"timeout\",\n142\t                    data={},\n143\t                    error=f\"Timed out after {timeout}s\",\n144\t                    duration_ms=int(timeout * 1000),\n145\t                )\n146\t                timed_out.append(source)\n147\t                if required:\n148\t                    caveats.append(f\"Required source {source.value} timed out\")\n149\t\n150\t            except Exception as e:\n151\t                logger.error(f\"Error fetching {source.value}: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n152\t                sources[source] = SourceResult(\n153\t                    source=source,\n154\t                    status=\"error\",\n155\t                    data={},\n156\t                    error=str(e),\n157\t                    duration_ms=0,\n158\t                )\n159\t                failed.append(source)\n160\t                if required:\n161\t                    caveats.append(f\"Required source {source.value} crashed: {e}\")\n162\t\n163\t        # Build coverage report\n164\t        coverage = FetchCoverage(\n165\t            total_sources=len(command_specs),\n166\t            succeeded=succeeded,\n167\t            timed_out=timed_out,\n168\t            failed=failed,\n169\t            skipped=skipped,\n170\t        )\n171\t\n172\t        total_duration_ms = int((time.time() - start_time) * 1000)\n173\t\n174\t        result = FetchResult(\n175\t            intent_id=request.intent_id,\n176\t            intent_type=request.intent_type,\n177\t            sources=sources,\n178\t            coverage=coverage,\n179\t            total_duration_ms=total_duration_ms,\n180\t            caveats=caveats if caveats else None,\n181\t        )\n182\t\n183\t        logger.info(\n184\t            f\"Fetch complete for intent {request.intent_id}: \"\n185\t            f\"{len(succeeded)}/{coverage.total_sources} succeeded, \"\n186\t            f\"{len(timed_out)} timed out, {len(failed)} failed\"\n187\t        )\n188\t\n189\t        # Detect if ALL sources failed - this is a terminal failure condition\n190\t        # The caller (intent router) should broadcast all_sources_failed event\n191\t        if len(succeeded) == 0 and coverage.total_sources > 0:\n192\t            logger.error(\n193\t                f\"All fetch sources failed for intent {request.intent_id}: \"\n194\t                f\"{len(failed)} failed, {len(timed_out)} timed out\"\n195\t            )\n196\t            # Mark result with terminal failure flag\n197\t            result.terminal_failure = \"all_sources_failed\"\n198\t\n199\t        return result\n200\t\n201\t    async def _execute_source(\n202\t        self,\n203\t        spec,\n204\t        context: FetchContext,\n205\t    ) -> SourceResult:\n206\t        \"\"\"Execute a single fetch source.\"\"\"\n207\t        executor = self._source_executors.get(spec.source)\n208\t        if not executor:\n209\t            return SourceResult(\n210\t                source=spec.source,\n211\t                status=\"error\",\n212\t                data={},\n213\t                error=f\"No executor for source {spec.source.value}\",\n214\t                duration_ms=0,\n215\t            )\n216\t\n217\t        start = time.time()\n218\t        try:\n219\t            data = await executor(context)\n220\t            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n221\t\n222\t            return SourceResult(\n223\t                source=spec.source,\n224\t                status=\"success\",\n225\t                data=data,\n226\t                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n227\t            )\n228\t\n229\t        except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n230\t            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n231\t            return SourceResult(\n232\t                source=spec.source,\n233\t                status=\"timeout\",\n234\t                data={},\n235\t                error=f\"Timed out after {spec.timeout_seconds}s\",\n236\t                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n237\t            )\n238\t\n239\t        except Exception as e:\n240\t            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n241\t            return SourceResult(\n242\t                source=spec.source,\n243\t                status=\"error\",\n244\t                data={},\n245\t                error=str(e),\n246\t                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n247\t            )\n248\t\n249\t    # Source executors\n250\t\n251\t    async def _fetch_kubectl_pods(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n252\t        \"\"\"Fetch pod status from kubernetes.\"\"\"\n253\t        proxy = context.proxy\n254\t        namespace = context.namespace\n255\t\n256\t        if not httpx.HTTPStatusError as e:\n296\t            if e.response.status_code == 404:\n297\t                return {\n298\t                    \"namespace\": namespace,\n299\t                    \"error\": resolved from the project's ``cluster`` via\n380\t        config/clusters.yaml (see src/fetch/clusters.py): there is no single\n381\t        ArgoCD API, and querying the wrong instance returns not-found —\n382\t        indistinguishable from \"app doesn't exist\". An unmapped cluster, or one\n383\t        mapped to an ``access`` mode the strand cannot satisfy (it holds no\n384\t        ArgoCD credentials, so only ``read-only-proxy`` is consumable), raises\n385\t        ``ArgocdEndpointUnresolvable`` *before* any HTTP call.\n386\t\n387\t        The exception is raised outside the httpx try/except so the resolution\n388\t        ``reason`` propagates cleanly: ``_execute_source`` buckets it as a\n389\t        failed source and the fetch loop emits a ``fetch_coverage`` caveat\n390\t        carrying the reason. Never a silent wrong-instance query.\n391\t        \"\"\"\n392\t        app_name = context.app_name or context.project_slug\n393\t        if not app_name:\n394\t            return {\"error\": \"No application name specified\"}\n395\t\n396\t        resolution = resolve_argocd_endpoint(context.cluster)\n397\t        if not resolution.satisfiable:\n398\t            raise ArgocdEndpointUnresolvable(\n399\t                resolution.reason or \"ArgoCD endpoint unresolvable\",\n400\t                cluster=context.cluster,\n401\t            )\n402\t\n403\t        proxy = resolution.argocd_api\n404\t        try:\n405\t            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0, verify=False) as client:\n406\t                resp = await client.get(\n407\t                    f\"{proxy}/api/v1/applications\",\n408\t                    params={\"name\": app_name}\n409\t                )\n410\t                resp.raise_for_status()\n411\t                apps_data =    \"name\": app_name,\n422\t                    \"sync_status\": status.get(\"sync\", {}).get(\"status\", \"Unknown\"),\n423\t                    \"health_status\": status.get(\"health\", {}).get(\"status\", \"Unknown\"),\n424\t                    \"revision\": status.get(\"sync\", {}).get(\"revision\", \"\"),\n425\t                    \"operation\": operation.get(\"operation\", {}),\n426\t                    \"created_at\": status.get(\"operationState\", {}).get(\"startedAt\", \"\"),\n427\t                }\n428\t\n429\t        except Exception as e:\n430\t            return {\"error\": str(e)}\n431\t\n432\t    def _make_cmd(self, context: FetchContext, *args: str) -> list[str]:\n433\t        \"\"\"Wrap a command with SSH if the repo is remote.\"\"\"\n434\t        if context.repo_path\n442\t        if not repo_path:\n443\t            return {\"error\": \"No repo path resolved\"}\n444\t\n445\t        if not context.ssh_target and not \"--pretty=format:%h|%s|%an|%ar\"),\n453\t                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n454\t                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n455\t            )\n456\t\n457\t            stdout,              commits.append({\n468\t                            \"hash\": parts[0],\n469\t                            \"message\": parts[1],\n470\t                            \"author\":                \"count\": len(commits),\n488\t            }\n489\t\n490\t        except Exception as e:\n491\t            return {\"error\": str(e), \"commits\": []}\n492\t\n493\t    async def _fetch_git_status(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n494\t        \"\"\"Fetch git status for a project (local or remote via SSH).\"\"\"\n495\t        repo_path = context.repo_path\n496\t        if           \"-C\", repo_path,\n506\t                \"status\", \"--short\",\n507\t                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n508\t                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n509\t            )\n510\t\n511\t            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n512\t\n513\t            if proc.returncode != 0:\n514\t                return {\"error\": stderr.decode()}\n515\t\n516\t \"-C\", repo_path,\n542\t                \"log\", \"-1\", \"--oneline\",\n543\t                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n544\t                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n545\t            )\n546\t            log_stdout, _ = await log_proc.communicate()\n547\t            last_commit = log_stdout.decode().strip()\n548\t\n549\t            return {\n550\t                \"repo\": \"changed_files\": []}\n560\t\n561\t    async def _fetch_bead_list(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n562\t        \"\"\"Fetch bead list from the repo's .beads/ workspace (local or FetchOrchestrator:\n848\t    \"\"\"\n849\t    Orchestrates concurrent fetch execution with streaming support.\n850\t\n851\t    This is a convenience wrapper around FetchStrand that provides the same interface.\n852\t    The actual fetch execution is handled by the FetchStrand instance.\n853\t\n854\t    Features:\n855\t    - Concurrent execution of all fetch sources\n856\t    - Streaming callbacks as sources complete\n857\t    - Per-source timeout enforcement\n858\t    - Coverage tracking (succeeded, timed_out, failed, skipped)\n859\t    - Caveat generation for failed sources\n860\t    \"\"\"\n861\t\n862\t    def __init__(self, fetch_strand: Optional[FetchStrand] = None):\n863\t        \"\"\"\n864\t        Initialize the orchestrator.\n865\t\n866\t        Args:\n867\t          self._strand = fetch_strand\n873\t\n874\t    async def execute_fetch(\n875\t        self,\n876\t        request: FetchRequest,\n877\t        stream_callback: Optional[StreamCallback] = with intent type and context\n884\t            stream_callback: Optional callback for incremental results\n885\t\n886\t        Returns:\n887\t            FetchResult with all source results and coverage info\n888\t        \"\"\"\n889\t        logger.info(\n890\t            f\"Starting fetch for intent {request.intent_id} \"\n891\t            f\"(type={request.intent_type.value})\"\n892\t        )\n893\t\n894\t        # Delegate to FetchStrand which handles:\n895\t        # - Concurrent execution\n896\t        # - Per-source timeouts\n897\t        # - Streaming callbacks\n898\t        # - logger.info(\n905\t            f\"Fetch complete for intent {request.intent_id}: \"\n906\t            f\"{result.coverage.success_rate:.1%} success, \"\n907\t            f\"{result.total_duration_ms}ms\"\n908\t        )\n909\t\n910\t        return result\n911\t\n912\t\n913\t# Global orchestrator instance\n914\t_orchestrator: Optional[FetchOrchestrator] = \"\"\"\n939\t    orchestrator = get_orchestrator()\n940\t    return await orchestrator.execute_fetch(request, stream_callback)\n941\t\n942\t\n943\t# Global fetch strand instance\n944\t_fetch_strand: _fetch_strand\n953\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"4f22ead5-b827-4364-bd26-beb675705de3","uuid":"b82eacd0-519f-489e-8d5c-b9b4d907d0e9","timestamp":"2026-07-23T23:18:50.323Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/src/fetch/orchestrator.py","content":"\"\"\"\nFetch orchestrator: coordinates concurrent fetch execution with streaming support.\n\nRuns all fetch sources concurrently, provides incremental results via callbacks,\ntracks coverage, and surfaces caveats for failed sources.\n\nThis is the canonical fetch implementation, consolidating the command matrix,\nconcurrent execution, and coverage tracking into a single module.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport asyncio\nimport time\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any, AsyncIterator, Callable, Optional\n\nimport httpx\n\nfrom .commands import (\n    FetchContext,\n    FetchCoverage,\n    FetchRequest,\n    FetchResult,\n    FetchSource,\n    IntentType,\n    KUBECTL_PROXIES,\n    get_fetch_commands,\n    get_required_sources,\n    SourceResult,\n)\nfrom .clusters import ArgocdEndpointUnresolvable, resolve_argocd_endpoint\n\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n# Type alias for the streaming callback\n# Called when a source completes: (source, result) -> None\nStreamCallback = Callable[[FetchSource, SourceResult], None]\n\n\nclass FetchStrand:\n    \"\"\"\n    Fetch strand: concurrent data fetcher with streaming support.\n\n    Executes all fetch commands for an intent concurrently, streams partial\n    results as they arrive, and provides comprehensive coverage tracking.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        self._source_executors = {\n            FetchSource.KUBECTL_PODS: self._fetch_kubectl_pods,\n            FetchSource.KUBECTL_DEPLOYMENTS: self._fetch_kubectl_deployments,\n            FetchSource.KUBECTL_WORKFLOWS: self._fetch_kubectl_workflows,\n            FetchSource.ARGOCD_APP: self._fetch_argocd_app,\n            FetchSource.GIT_LOG: self._fetch_git_log,\n            FetchSource.GIT_STATUS: self._fetch_git_status,\n            FetchSource.BEAD_LIST: self._fetch_bead_list,\n            FetchSource.BEAD_DETAILS: self._fetch_bead_details,\n            FetchSource.CI_STATUS: self._fetch_ci_status,\n            FetchSource.COMPONENTS: self._fetch_components,\n            FetchSource.LOGS: self._fetch_logs,\n            FetchSource.EVENTS: self._fetch_events,\n            FetchSource.SESSION_STATE: self._fetch_session_state,\n            FetchSource.TOPIC_CONTEXT: self._fetch_topic_context,\n            FetchSource.REMINDERS: self._fetch_reminders,\n            FetchSource.FS_EXPLORE: self._fetch_fs_explore,\n            FetchSource.FS_README: self._fetch_fs_readme,\n            FetchSource.FS_HOME: self._fetch_fs_home,\n        }\n\n    async def fetch(\n        self,\n        request: FetchRequest,\n        on_partial_result: Optional[Callable[[FetchSource, SourceResult], None]] = None,\n    ) -> FetchResult:\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute fetch request with optional streaming callback.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The fetch request to execute\n            on_partial_result: Optional callback for streaming partial results\n\n        Returns:\n            FetchResult with coverage information\n        \"\"\"\n        start_time = time.time()\n\n        # Get fetch commands for this intent type\n        command_specs = get_fetch_commands(request.intent_type)\n        required_sources = get_required_sources(request.intent_type)\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetching {len(command_specs)} sources for intent {request.intent_id} \"\n            f\"(type: {request.intent_type.value})\"\n        )\n\n        # Create tasks for concurrent execution\n        tasks = []\n        for spec in command_specs:\n            task = asyncio.create_task(\n                self._execute_source(spec, request.context),\n                name=f\"fetch_{spec.source.value}_{request.intent_id[:8]}\",\n            )\n            tasks.append((spec.source, spec.required, spec.timeout_seconds, task))\n\n        # Wait for all tasks to complete (with per-task timeout)\n        sources = {}\n        succeeded = []\n        timed_out = []\n        failed = []\n        skipped = []\n        caveats = []\n\n        for source, required, timeout, task in tasks:\n            try:\n                result = await asyncio.wait_for(task, timeout=timeout)\n\n                if on_partial_result:\n                    # Stream partial result as it arrives\n                    on_partial_result(source, result)\n\n                sources[source] = result\n\n                if result.status == \"success\":\n                    succeeded.append(source)\n                elif result.status == \"timeout\":\n                    timed_out.append(source)\n                    caveats.append(f\"{source.value} timed out\")\n                elif result.status == \"error\":\n                    failed.append(source)\n                    if required:\n                        caveats.append(f\"Required source {source.value} failed: {result.error}\")\n                    else:\n                        caveats.append(f\"Optional source {source.value} failed: {result.error}\")\n\n            except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n                logger.warning(f\"Source {source.value} timed out after {timeout}s\")\n                sources[source] = SourceResult(\n                    source=source,\n                    status=\"timeout\",\n                    data={},\n                    error=f\"Timed out after {timeout}s\",\n                    duration_ms=int(timeout * 1000),\n                )\n                timed_out.append(source)\n                if required:\n                    caveats.append(f\"Required source {source.value} timed out\")\n\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Error fetching {source.value}: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n                sources[source] = SourceResult(\n                    source=source,\n                    status=\"error\",\n                    data={},\n                    error=str(e),\n                    duration_ms=0,\n                )\n                failed.append(source)\n                if required:\n                    caveats.append(f\"Required source {source.value} crashed: {e}\")\n\n        # Build coverage report\n        coverage = FetchCoverage(\n            total_sources=len(command_specs),\n            succeeded=succeeded,\n            timed_out=timed_out,\n            failed=failed,\n            skipped=skipped,\n        )\n\n        total_duration_ms = int((time.time() - start_time) * 1000)\n\n        result = FetchResult(\n            intent_id=request.intent_id,\n            intent_type=request.intent_type,\n            sources=sources,\n            coverage=coverage,\n            total_duration_ms=total_duration_ms,\n            caveats=caveats if caveats else None,\n        )\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetch complete for intent {request.intent_id}: \"\n            f\"{len(succeeded)}/{coverage.total_sources} succeeded, \"\n            f\"{len(timed_out)} timed out, {len(failed)} failed\"\n        )\n\n        # Detect if ALL sources failed - this is a terminal failure condition\n        # The caller (intent router) should broadcast all_sources_failed event\n        if len(succeeded) == 0 and coverage.total_sources > 0:\n            logger.error(\n                f\"All fetch sources failed for intent {request.intent_id}: \"\n                f\"{len(failed)} failed, {len(timed_out)} timed out\"\n            )\n            # Mark result with terminal failure flag\n            result.terminal_failure = \"all_sources_failed\"\n\n        return result\n\n    async def _execute_source(\n        self,\n        spec,\n        context: FetchContext,\n    ) -> SourceResult:\n        \"\"\"Execute a single fetch source.\"\"\"\n        executor = self._source_executors.get(spec.source)\n        if not executor:\n            return SourceResult(\n                source=spec.source,\n                status=\"error\",\n                data={},\n                error=f\"No executor for source {spec.source.value}\",\n                duration_ms=0,\n            )\n\n        start = time.time()\n        try:\n            data = await executor(context)\n            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n\n            return SourceResult(\n                source=spec.source,\n                status=\"success\",\n                data=data,\n                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n            )\n\n        except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n            return SourceResult(\n                source=spec.source,\n                status=\"timeout\",\n                data={},\n                error=f\"Timed out after {spec.timeout_seconds}s\",\n                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n            )\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n            return SourceResult(\n                source=spec.source,\n                status=\"error\",\n                data={},\n                error=str(e),\n                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n            )\n\n    # Source executors\n\n    async def _fetch_kubectl_pods(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch pod status from kubernetes.\"\"\"\n        proxy = context.proxy\n        namespace = context.namespace\n\n        if not namespace:\n            # Try to derive from project_slug\n            if context.project_slug:\n                namespace = context.project_slug.replace(\"-\", \"\")\n            else:\n                return {\"error\": \"No namespace specified\"}\n\n        try:\n            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0) as client:\n                resp = await client.get(\n                    f\"{proxy}/api/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/pods\"\n                )\n                resp.raise_for_status()\n                pods_data = resp.json()\n\n                pods = []\n                for pod in pods_data.get(\"items\", []):\n                    pod_name = pod.get(\"metadata\", {}).get(\"name\", \"\")\n                    phase = pod.get(\"status\", {}).get(\"phase\", \"Unknown\")\n                    ready = self._get_pod_ready(pod)\n                    restarts = sum(\n                        c.get(\"restartCount\", 0)\n                        for c in pod.get(\"status\", {}).get(\"containerStatuses\", [])\n                    )\n\n                    pods.append({\n                        \"name\": pod_name,\n                        \"phase\": phase,\n                        \"ready\": ready,\n                        \"restarts\": restarts,\n                    })\n\n                return {\n                    \"namespace\": namespace,\n                    \"pods\": pods,\n                    \"pod_count\": len(pods),\n                    \"healthy_count\": sum(1 for p in pods if p[\"phase\"] == \"Running\"),\n                }\n\n        except httpx.HTTPStatusError as e:\n            if e.response.status_code == 404:\n                return {\n                    \"namespace\": namespace,\n                    \"error\": \"Namespace not found\",\n                    \"pods\": [],\n                }\n            raise\n        except Exception as e:\n            return {\"error\": str(e), \"pods\": []}\n\n    async def _fetch_kubectl_deployments(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch deployment status from kubernetes.\"\"\"\n        proxy = context.proxy\n        namespace = context.namespace\n        deployment = context.deployment or context.app_name\n\n        if not namespace or not deployment:\n            return {\"error\": \"Missing namespace or deployment name\"}\n\n        try:\n            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0) as client:\n                resp = await client.get(\n                    f\"{proxy}/apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/deployments/{deployment}\"\n                )\n                resp.raise_for_status()\n                deploy_data = resp.json()\n\n                status = deploy_data.get(\"status\", {})\n                spec = deploy_data.get(\"spec\", {})\n\n                return {\n                    \"name\": deployment,\n                    \"namespace\": namespace,\n                    \"replicas\": spec.get(\"replicas\", 0),\n                    \"ready_replicas\": status.get(\"readyReplicas\", 0),\n                    \"available_replicas\": status.get(\"availableReplicas\", 0),\n                    \"updated_replicas\": status.get(\"updatedReplicas\", 0),\n                    \"conditions\": _fetch_argocd_app(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch ArgoCD application status.\n\n        The ArgoCD endpoint is resolved from the project's ``cluster`` via\n        config/clusters.yaml (see src/fetch/clusters.py): there is no single\n        ArgoCD API, and querying the wrong instance returns not-found —\n        indistinguishable from \"app doesn't exist\". An unmapped cluster, or one\n        mapped to an ``access`` mode the strand cannot satisfy (it holds no\n        ArgoCD credentials, so only ``read-only-proxy`` is consumable), raises\n        ``ArgocdEndpointUnresolvable`` *before* any HTTP call.\n\n        The exception is raised outside the httpx try/except so the resolution\n        ``reason`` propagates cleanly: ``_execute_source`` buckets it as a\n        failed source and the fetch loop emits a ``fetch_coverage`` caveat\n        carrying the reason. Never a silent wrong-instance query.\n        \"\"\"\n        app_name = context.app_name or context.project_slug\n        if not app_name:\n            return {\"error\": \"No application name specified\"}\n\n        resolution = resolve_argocd_endpoint(context.cluster)\n        if not resolution.satisfiable:\n            raise ArgocdEndpointUnresolvable(\n                resolution.reason or \"ArgoCD endpoint unresolvable\",\n                cluster=context.cluster,\n            )\n\n        proxy = resolution.argocd_api\n        try:\n            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0, verify=False) as client:\n                resp = await client.get(\n                    f\"{proxy}/api/v1/applications\",\n                    params={\"name\": app_name}\n                )\n                resp.raise_for_status()\n                apps_data = resp.json()\n\n                if not apps_data.get(\"items\"):\n                    return {\"error\": f\"Application not found: {app_name}\"}\n\n                app = apps_data[\"items\"][0]\n                status = app.get(\"status\", {})\n                operation = app.get(\"operation\", {})\n\n                return {\n                    \"name\": app_name,\n                    \"sync_status\": status.get(\"sync\", {}).get(\"status\", \"Unknown\"),\n                    \"health_status\": status.get(\"health\", {}).get(\"status\", \"Unknown\"),\n                    \"revision\": status.get(\"sync\", {}).get(\"revision\", \"\"),\n                    \"operation\": operation.get(\"operation\", {}),\n                    \"created_at\": status.get(\"operationState\", {}).get(\"startedAt\", \"\"),\n                }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            return {\"error\": str(e)}\n\n    def _make_cmd(self, context: FetchContext, *args: str) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Wrap a command with SSH if the repo is remote.\"\"\"\n        if context.ssh_target:\n            return [\"ssh\", \"-o\", \"ConnectTimeout=8\", \"-o\", \"BatchMode=yes\",\n                    context.ssh_target, \" \".join(args)]\n        return list(args)\n\n    async def _fetch_git_log(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch git log for a project (local or remote via SSH).\"\"\"\n        repo_path = context.repo_path\n        if not repo_path:\n            return {\"error\": \"No repo path resolved\"}\n\n        if not context.ssh_target and not Path(repo_path).exists():\n            return {\"error\": f\"Repo path not found: {repo_path}\"}\n\n        try:\n            # Fetch commits with author and date\n            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                *self._make_cmd(context, \"git\", \"-C\", repo_path, \"log\", \"-10\",\n                                \"--pretty=format:%h|%s|%an|%ar\"),\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n\n            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n            if proc.returncode != 0:\n                return {\"error\": stderr.decode()}\n\n            commits = []\n commits.append({\n                            \"hash\": parts[0],\n                            \"message\": parts[1],\n                            \"author\": parts[2],\n                            \"date\": parts[3],\n                        })\n\n            # Get current branch\n            branch_proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                *self._make_cmd(context, \"git\", \"commits\": commits,\n                \"count\": len(commits),\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            return {\"error\": str(e), \"commits\": []}\n\n    async def _get_pod_ready(self, pod_data: dict) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Get pod ready status (e.g., '1/1').\"\"\"\n        container_statuses = pod_data.get(\"status\", {}).get(\"containerStatuses\", [])\n FetchStrand that provides the same interface.\n    The actual fetch execution is handled by the FetchStrand instance.\n\n    Features:\n    - Concurrent execution of all fetch sources\n    - Streaming callbacks as sources complete\n    - Per-source timeout enforcement\n    - Coverage tracking (succeeded, timed_out, failed, skipped)\n    - Caveat generation for failed sources\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, fetch_strand: Optional[FetchStrand] = None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Initialize the orchestrator.\n\n        Args:\n            fetch_strand: FetchStrand instance (uses global if not provided)\n        \"\"\"\n        if fetch_strand is None:\n            self._strand = FetchStrand()\n        else:\n            self._strand = fetch_strand\n\n    async def execute_fetch(\n        self,\n        request: FetchRequest,\n        stream_callback: Optional[StreamCallback] = None,\n    ) -> FetchResult:\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute a fetch request with concurrent source execution.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The fetch request with intent type and context\n            stream_callback: Optional callback for incremental results\n\n        Returns:\n            FetchResult with all source results and coverage info\n        \"\"\"\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Starting fetch for intent {request.intent_id} \"\n            f\"(type={request.intent_type.value})\"\n        )\n\n        # Delegate to FetchStrand which handles:\n        # - Concurrent execution\n        # - Per-source timeouts\n        # - Streaming callbacks\n        # - Coverage tracking\n        result = await self._strand.fetch(\n            request=request,\n            on_partial_result=stream_callback,\n        )\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetch complete for intent {request.intent_id}: \"\n            f\"{result.coverage.success_rate:.1%} success, \"\n            f\"{result.total_duration_ms}ms\"\n        )\n\n        return result\n\n\n# Global orchestrator instance\n_orchestrator: Optional[FetchOrchestrator] = None\n\n\ndef get_orchestrator() -> FetchOrchestrator:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global orchestrator instance.\"\"\"\n    global _orchestrator\n    if _orchestrator is None:\n        _orchestrator = FetchOrchestrator()\n    return _orchestrator\n\n\nasync def execute_fetch(\n    request:  \"\"\"Intent types that the router can classify.\"\"\"\n69\t    STATUS = \"status\"\n70\t    ACTION = \"action\"\n71\t    BRAINSTORM = \"brainstorm\"\n72\t    LOOKUP = \"lookup\"\n73\t    REMINDER = \"reminder\"\n74\t    SELF_MODIFICATION = \"self-modification\"\n75\t    MONITORING_CONFIG = \"monitoring-config\"\n76\t    TASK_PROFILE = \"task-profile\"  # Escalate to NEEDLE bead\n77\t    CLARIFICATION = \"clarification\"  # Needs user input\n78\t    STUCK = \"stuck\"  # Stuck intent - task blocked by circuit breaker\n79\t\n80\t\n81\t@dataclass\n82\tclass IntentClassification:\n83\t    \"\"\"Result of intent classification.\"\"\"\n84\t    intent_type: IntentType\n85\t    project_slug: str | None = None\n86\t    confidence: float = 1.0\n87\t    utterance_fragment: str = \"\"\n88\t    reasoning: str = \"\"\n89\t    urgency: str = \"normal\"  # critical, high, normal, low\n90\t    lookup_kind: str | None = None  # lookup intents only: 'logs' | 'config' | 'docs'\n91\t\n92\t\n93\t@dataclass\n94\tclass RoutedIntent:\n95\t    \"\"\"A routed intent ready for processing.\"\"\"\n96\t    intent_id: str\n97\t    classification: IntentClassification\n98\t    session_id: str\n99\t    utterance: str\n100\t    # router_ms is measured once around classify_utterance() in route_utterance\n101\t    # (one LLM call per utterance) and shared across every intent thread from\n102\t    # that utterance — see Latency Budget & Instrumentation in docs/plan/plan.md.\n103\t    router_ms: int | None = None\n104\t\n105\t\n106\t# Path to the router segmentation prompt. Read from disk on each classify_utterance()\n107\t# call so edits to prompts/router.md take effect without a server restart (hot-reload),\n108\t# matching the pattern in src/synthesize/strand.py (SYNTHESIZE_PROMPT_PATH).\n109\tROUTER_PROMPT_PATH = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/prompts/router.md\")\n110\t\n111\t# Fallback used only if the prompt file cannot be read at runtime.\n112\t_ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK = (\n113\t    \"You are the Intent Router for aide-de-camp. Segment the utterance into \"\n114\t    \"distinct intent threads, classify each, and return ONLY a JSON array of \"\n115\t    \"intent objects.\"\n116\t)\n117\t\n118\t\n119\tclass IntentRouter:\n120\t    \"\"\"\n121\t    Intent Router classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n122\t\n123\t    For task-profile intents, routes to escalate strand for bead creation.\n124\t    For other intents, routes to fetch + synthesize strands (TODO).\n125\t    \"\"\"\n126\t\n127\t    def __init__(self, store=None, prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None):\n128\t        self.store = store\n129\t        self.prompt_path = prompt_path or ROUTER_PROMPT_PATH\n130\t        self._zai_client = None\n131\t        self._reload_manager = None\n132\t\n133\t    async def self._zai_client\n138\t\n139\t    async def _get_store(self):\n140\t        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n141\t        if self.store is None:\n142\t            self.store = get_store()\n143\t        return self.store\n144\t\n145\t    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n146\t        \"\"\"Get or create the hot-reload manager (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n147\t        if self._reload_manager is None:\n148\t            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n149\t        return self._reload_manager\n150\t\n151\t    def _load_router_prompt(self) -> str:\n152\t        \"\"\"Load the router segmentation prompt from disk (hot-reload, per call).\"\"\"\n153\t        try:\n154\t            return self.prompt_path.read_text()\n155\t        except Exception as e:\n156\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to load router prompt from {self.prompt_path}: {e}\")\n157\t            return _ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n158\t\n159\t    def _load_urgency_prompt(self) -> str:\n160\t        \"\"\"\n161\t        Load the urgency classification prompt from disk via the hot-reload manager.\n162\t\n163\t        prompts/urgency.md is a separately hot-reloadable artifact (registered in\n164\t        src/components/hot_reload.py). Splicing it here keeps urgency guidance\n165\t        editable independently of the segmentation prompt. Returns \"\" on failure\n166\t        so the router still functions without urgency guidance.\n167\t        \"\"\"\n168\t        try:\n169\t            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"urgency\")\n170\t        except Exception as e:\n171\t            logger.warning(f\"Failed to load urgency prompt: {e}\")\n172\t            return \"\"\n173\t\n174\t    def _build_system_prompt(self) -> str:\n175\t        \"\"\"Build the full system prompt: segmentation prompt + urgency rules.\"\"\"\n176\t        system_prompt = self._load_router_prompt()\n177\t        urgency_prompt = self._load_urgency_prompt()\n178\t        if urgency_prompt:\n179\t            system_prompt = (\n180\t                f\"{system_prompt}\\n\\n## Urgency Classification Rules\\n\\n{urgency_prompt}\"\n181\t            )\n182\t        return system_prompt\n183\t\n184\t    async def classify_utterance(\n185\t        self,\n186\t        utterance: str,\n187\t        session_id: str,\n188\t        retry_on_malformed: bool = True,\n189\t    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n190\t        \"\"\"\n191\t        Classify an utterance into intents.\n192\t\n193\t        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n194\t\n195\t        Args:\n196\t            utterance: The user utterance\n197\t            session_id: Session ID for context\n198\t            retry_on_malformed: If True, perform one corrective retry on JSON parse failure\n199\t\n200\t        Returns:\n201\t            List of IntentClassification objects\n202\t\n203\t        Raises:\n204\t            RouterTimeoutError: LLM call timed out\n205\t            RouterQuotaError: LLM quota exhausted\n206\t            RouterProxyError: LLM proxy unreachable\n207\t            RouterMalformedError: Malformed JSON after corrective retry\n208\t        \"\"\"\n209\t        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n210\t\n211\t        # Build user message with session context if available\n212\t        store = await self._get_store()\n213\t        session_context = \"\"\n214\t        try:\n215\t            session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n216\t            if session:\n217\t                # Get recent intents for context\n218\t                recent_intents = await store.get_recent_intents(session_id, limit=5)\n219\t                if recent_intents:\n220\t                    session_context = \"\\n\\nRecent intents in this session:\\n\"\n221\t                    for intent in recent_intents:\n222\t                        session_context += f\"- {intent.get('utterance', '')} ({intent.get('intent_type', 'unknown')})\\n\"\n223\t        except Exception as e:\n224\t            logger.warning(f\"Failed to get session context: {e}\")\n225\t\n226\t        user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}{session_context}\"\n227\t\n228\t        logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n229\t\n230\t        # Build system prompt per call from prompts/router.md (+ urgency rules),\n231\t        # so edits to either prompt take effect without a server restart.\n232\t        system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n233\t\n234\t        # Track retry attempt for malformed JSON\n235\t        retry_count = 0\n236\t        raw_response = None\n237\t\n238\t        try:\n239\t            response = await client.call_simple(\n240\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n241\t                user_message=user_message,\n242\t                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n243\t                max_tokens=2048,\n244\t                temperature=0.3,  # Lower temperature for consistent classification\n245\t            )\n246\t\n247\t            # Store raw response for error reporting\n248\t            raw_response = response\n249\t\n250\t            # Strip markdown code fences if present (ZAI proxy wraps in ```json...```)\n251\t            raw = response.strip()\n252\t            if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n253\t                raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n254\t                raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n255\t\n256\t            # Parse JSON response\n257\t            intents_data = json.loads(raw)\n258\t            classifications = []\n259\t\n260\t            for intent_data in intents_data:\n261\t                # Map string to IntentType enum\n262\t                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n263\t                try:\n264\t                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n265\t                except ValueError:\n266\t                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n267\t                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n268\t\n269\t                classification = IntentClassification(\n270\t                    intent_type=intent_type,\n271\t                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n272\t                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n273\t                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n274\t                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n275\t                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n276\t                    lookup_kind=intent_data.get(\"lookup_kind\") if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP else None,\n277\t                )\n278\t                classifications.append(classification)\n279\t\n280\t            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n281\t            return classifications\n282\t\n283\t        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n284\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response as JSON: {e}\")\n285\t\n286\t            # Implement one corrective retry on malformed JSON\n287\t            if retry_on_malformed and retry_count == 0:\n288\t                logger.info(\"Malformed JSON detected, attempting corrective retry...\")\n289\t                retry_count += 1\n290\t                try:\n291\t                    # Retry once with same parameters\n292\t                    return await self.classify_utterance(\n293\t                        utterance=utterance,\n294\t                        session_id=session_id,\n295\t                        retry_on_malformed=False,  # Prevent infinite retry\n296\t                    )\n297\t                except json.JSONDecode as retry_e:\n298\t                    # Retry also failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n299\t                    raise RouterMalformedError(\n300\t                        parse_error=str(e),\n301\t                        raw_output=raw_response or \"\",\n302\t                        retry_count=retry_count,\n303\t                    ) from retry_e\n304\t\n305\t            # No retry or retry failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n306\t            raise RouterMalformedError(\n307\t                parse_error=str(e),\n308\t                raw_output=raw_response or \"\",\n309\t                retry_count=retry_count,\n310\t            ) from e\n311\t\n312\t        except (LLMTimeoutError, asyncio.TimeoutError) as e:\n313\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\")\n314\t            raise RouterTimeoutError(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\") from e\n315\t\n316\t        except (LLMRateLimitError,) as e:\n317\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\")\n318\t            raise RouterQuotaError(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\") from e\n319\t\n320\t        except (LLMError, httpx.HTTPError) as e:\n321\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\")\n322\t            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\") from e\n323\t\n324\t        except Exception as e:\n325\t            logger.error(f\"Classification failed with unexpected error: {e}\")\n326\t            # Wrap unknown errors as RouterProxyError for graceful degradation\n327\t            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM call failed: {e}\") from e\n328\t\n329\t    async def route_utterance(\n330\t        self,\n331\t        utterance: str,\n332\t        utterance_id: str,\n333\t        session_id: str,\n334\t    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n335\t        \"\"\"\n336\t        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n337\t\n338\t        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n339\t\n340\t        Args:\n341\t            utterance: The user utterance\n342\t            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n343\t            session_id: Session ID\n344\t\n345\t        Returns:\n346\t            List of RoutedIntent objects\n347\t\n348\t        Raises:\n349\t            RouterError: If router fails and broadcasts error event\n350\t        \"\"\"\n351\t        # Generate a fallback intent_id for error reporting\n352\t        error_intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n353\t\n354\t        try:\n355\t            # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n356\t            # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n357\t            # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n358\t            classify_start = time.monotonic()\n359\t            classifications = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n360\t            router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n361\t\n362\t            routed_intents = []\n363\t            for classification in classifications:\n364\t                # Create intent ID\n365\t                intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n366\t\n367\t                routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n368\t                    intent_id=intent_id,\n369\t                    classification=classification,\n370\t                    session_id=session_id,\n371\t                    utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n372\t                    router_ms=router_ms,\n373\t                )\n374\t                routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n375\t\n376\t            return routed_intents\n377\t\n378\t        except RouterMalformedError as e:\n379\t            # Malformed JSON after corrective retry - broadcast clarification card\n380\t            logger.error(f\"Router malformed after retry: {e.parse_error}\")\n381\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n382\t            await handler.broadcast_clarification_card(\n383\t                utterance=utterance,\n384\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n385\t                session_id=session_id,\n386\t                parse_error=e.parse_error,\n387\t                retry_count=e.retry_count,\n388\t                raw_output_snippet=e.raw_output[:200] if e.raw_output else None,\n389\t            )\n390\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n391\t\n392\t        except RouterTimeoutError as e:\n393\t            # Router LLM timeout - broadcast router_unavailable\n394\t            logger.error(f\"Router timeout: {e}\")\n395\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n396\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n397\t                utterance=utterance,\n398\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n399\t                session_id=session_id,\n400\t                error_reason=\"timeout\",\n401\t            )\n402\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n403\t\n404\t        except RouterQuotaError as e:\n405\t            # Router quota exhausted - broadcast router_unavailable\n406\t            logger.error(f\"Router quota exhausted: {e}\")\n407\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n408\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n409\t                utterance=utterance,\n410\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n411\t                session_id=session_id,\n412\t                error_reason=\"quota_exhausted\",\n413\t            )\n414\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n415\t\n416\t        except RouterProxyError as e:\n417\t            # Router proxy unreachable - broadcast router_unavailable\n418\t            logger.error(f\"Router proxy error: {e}\")\n419\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n420\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n421\t                utterance=utterance,\n422\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n423\t                session_id=session_id,\n424\t                error_reason=\"proxy_down\",\n425\t            )\n426\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n427\t\n428\t    async def process_intent(\n429\t        self,\n430\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n431\t    ) -> dict:\n432\t        \"\"\"\n433\t        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n434\t\n435\t        Args:\n436\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n437\t\n438\t        Returns:\n439\t            Result dictionary with status and data\n440\t        \"\"\"\n441\t        classification = routed_intent.classification\n442\t\n443\t        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n444\t        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n445\t        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n446\t        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n447\t        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n448\t        # the dispatch itself.\n449\t        timings = DispatchTimings()\n450\t        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n451\t\n452\t        try:\n453\t            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n454\t            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n455\t                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n456\t            else:\n457\t                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n458\t                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n459\t        except Exception:\n460\t            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n461\t            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n462\t            raise\n463\t\n464\t        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n465\t        return result\n466\t\n467\t    async def _persist_timings(\n468\t        self,\n469\t        intent_id: str,\n470\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n471\t    ) -> None:\n472\t        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n473\t        try:\n474\t            store = await self._get_store()\n475\t            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n476\t        except Exception as e:\n477\t            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n478\t\n479\t    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n480\t        self,\n481\t Args:\n488\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n489\t            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records fetch_first_source_ms,\n490\t                fetch_total_ms, and {classification.intent_type.value})\"\n500\t        )\n501\t\n502\t        try:\n503\t            # Step 1: Fetch context — resolve project slug to local repo path\n504\t            fetch_intent_type location = entry.display_path\n522\t                    logger.info(f\"Resolved '{classification.project_slug}' → {location}\")\n523\t                else:\n524\t                    logger.info(f\"No )\n534\t\n535\t            fetch_request = FetchRequest(\n536\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n537\t                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n538\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n539\t                context=FetchContext(\n540\t                    project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n541\t                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n542\t                    repo_path=repo_path,\n543\t                    ssh_target=ssh_target,\n544\t                    host_alias=host_alias,\n545\t                    cluster=project_cfg.get(\"cluster\") if project_cfg else None,\n546\t                    namespace=project_cfg.get(\"namespace\") if project_cfg else None,\n547\t                    app_name=project_cfg.get(\"argocd_app\") if project_cfg else None,\n548\t                ),\n549\t            )\n550\t\n551\t            # fetch_first_source_ms = time from fetch start to the first source\n552\t            # resolving (success/fail/timeout — the first progress state on the\n553\t            # pending card). fetch_total_ms = the fetch window close.\n554\t            fetch_start = timings.clock()\n555\t            first_source_at: list[float | None] = [None]\n556\t\n557\t            def _on_first_source(_source, _result) -> None:\n558\t                if first_source_at[0] is timings.record(\"fetch_total_ms\", fetch_result.total_duration_ms)\n563\t            if first_source_at[0] is not None:\n564\t                timings.record(\n565\t                    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n566\t                    timings.elapsed_ms(fetch_start, first_source_at[0]),\n567\t                )\n568\t\n569\t            # Check for terminal failure: ALL sources failed (Degraded-State UX)\n570\t            if fetch_result.terminal_failure == \"all_sources_failed\":\n571\t                logger.error(\n572\t                    f\"All fetch sources failed for               )\n576\t\n577\t                # Build failed sources list for error event\n578\t                failed_sources = []\n579\t                for source, result in fetch_result.sources.items():\n580\t                    failed_sources.append({\n581\t                        \"source\": source.value,\n582\t                        \"status\": result.status,\n583\t                        \"error\": result.error,\n584\t                    })\n585\t\n586\t                # Broadcast all_sources_failed error event\n587\t                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n588\t                await handler.broadcast_all_sources_failed(\n589\t                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n590\t                    intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n591\t                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n592\t                    utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n593\t                    failed_sources=failed_sources,\n594\t                )\n595\t\n596\t                # Update intent status to failed\n597\t                store = get_store()\n598\t                await )\n603\t\n604\t                return {\n605\t                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n606\t                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n607\t                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n608\t                    \"error\": \"all_sources_failed\",\n609\t                    \"message\": \"No data — all required sources failed\",\n610\t                    \"terminal_failure\": \"all_sources_failed\",\n611\t                }\n612\t\n613\t            # Check for required source failures (terminal condition)\n614\t            required_sources_failed =
