get_broadcaster, SSEEvent, EventType\n\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n\n# Router error types for degraded-state handling\nclass RouterError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"Base exception for router errors.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass RouterTimeoutError(RouterError):\n    \"\"\"Router LLM call timed out.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass RouterQuotaError(RouterError):\n    \"\"\"Router LLM call quota exhausted.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass RouterProxyError(RouterError):\n    \"\"\"Router LLM proxy unreachable.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass RouterMalformedError(RouterError):\n    \"\"\"Router returned malformed JSON (after corrective retry).\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, parse_error: str, raw_output: str, retry_count: int = 0):\n        self.parse_error = parse_error\n        self.raw_output = raw_output\n        self.retry_count = retry_count\n        super().__init__(f\"Malformed router output (retry {retry_count}): {parse_error}\")\n\n\nclass IntentType(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Intent types that the router can classify.\"\"\"\n    STATUS = \"status\"\n    ACTION = \"action\"\n    BRAINSTORM = \"brainstorm\"\n    LOOKUP = \"lookup\"\n    REMINDER = \"reminder\"\n    SELF_MODIFICATION = \"self-modification\"\n    MONITORING_CONFIG = \"monitoring-config\"\n    TASK_PROFILE = \"task-profile\"  # Escalate to NEEDLE bead\n    CLARIFICATION = \"clarification\"  # Needs user input\n    STUCK = \"stuck\"  # Stuck intent - task blocked by circuit breaker\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass IntentClassification:\n    \"\"\"Result of intent classification.\"\"\"\n    intent_type: IntentType\n    project_slug: str | None = None\n    confidence: float = 1.0\n    utterance_fragment: str = \"\"\n    reasoning: str = \"\"\n    urgency: str = \"normal\"  # critical, high, normal, low\n    lookup_kind: str | None = None  # lookup intents only: 'logs' | 'config' | 'docs'\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass RoutedIntent:\n    \"\"\"A routed intent ready for processing.\"\"\"\n    intent_id: str\n    classification: IntentClassification\n    session_id: str\n    utterance: str\n    # router_ms is measured once around classify_utterance() in route_utterance\n    # (one LLM call per utterance) and shared across every intent thread from\n    # that utterance — see Latency Budget & Instrumentation in docs/plan/plan.md.\n    router_ms: int | None = None\n\n\n# Path to the router segmentation prompt. Read from disk on each classify_utterance()\n# call so edits to prompts/router.md take effect without a server restart (hot-reload),\n# matching the pattern in src/synthesize/strand.py (SYNTHESIZE_PROMPT_PATH).\nROUTER_PROMPT_PATH = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/prompts/router.md\")\n\n# Fallback used only if the prompt file cannot be read at runtime.\n_ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK = (\n    \"You are the Intent Router for aide-de-camp. Segment the utterance into \"\n    \"distinct intent threads, classify each, and return ONLY a JSON array of \"\n    \"intent objects.\"\n)\n\n\nclass IntentRouter:\n    \"\"\"\n    Intent Router classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n\n    For task-profile intents, routes to escalate strand for bead creation.\n    For other intents, routes to fetch + synthesize strands (TODO).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, store=None, prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None):\n        self.store = store\n        self.prompt_path = prompt_path or ROUTER_PROMPT_PATH\n        self._zai_client = None\n        self._reload_manager = None\n\n    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n        if self._zai_client is None:\n            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n        return self._zai_client\n\n    async def _get_store(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n        if self.store is None:\n            self.store = get_store()\n        return self.store\n\n    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create the hot-reload manager (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n        if self._reload_manager is None:\n            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n        return self._reload_manager\n\n    def _load_router_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Load the router segmentation prompt from disk (hot-reload, per call).\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self.prompt_path.read_text()\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to load router prompt from {self.prompt_path}: {e}\")\n            return _ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n\n    def _load_urgency_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Load the urgency classification prompt from disk via the hot-reload manager.\n\n        prompts/urgency.md is a separately hot-reloadable artifact (registered in\n        src/components/hot_reload.py). Splicing it here keeps urgency guidance\n        editable independently of the segmentation prompt. Returns \"\" on failure\n        so the router still functions without urgency guidance.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"urgency\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to load urgency prompt: {e}\")\n            return \"\"\n\n    def _build_system_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Build the full system prompt: segmentation prompt + urgency rules.\"\"\"\n        system_prompt = self._load_router_prompt()\n        urgency_prompt = self._load_urgency_prompt()\n        if urgency_prompt:\n            system_prompt = (\n                f\"{system_prompt}\\n\\n## Urgency Classification Rules\\n\\n{urgency_prompt}\"\n            )\n        return system_prompt\n\n    async def classify_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        retry_on_malformed: bool = True,\n    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Classify an utterance into intents.\n\n        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            session_id: Session ID for context\n            retry_on_malformed: If True, perform one corrective retry on JSON parse failure\n\n        Returns:\n            List of IntentClassification objects\n\n        Raises:\n            RouterTimeoutError: LLM call timed out\n            RouterQuotaError: LLM quota exhausted\n            RouterProxyError: LLM proxy unreachable\n            RouterMalformedError: Malformed JSON after corrective retry\n        \"\"\"\n        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n\n        # Build user message with session context if available\n        store = await self._get_store()\n        session_context = \"\"\n        try:\n            session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n            if session:\n                # Get recent intents for context\n                recent_intents = await store.get_recent_intents(session_id, limit=5)\n                if recent_intents:\n                    session_context = \"\\n\\nRecent intents in this session:\\n\"\n                    for intent in recent_intents:\n                        session_context += f\"- {intent.get('utterance', '')} ({intent.get('intent_type', 'unknown')})\\n\"\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to get session context: {e}\")\n\n        user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}{session_context}\"\n\n        logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n\n        # Build system prompt per call from prompts/router.md (+ urgency rules),\n        # so edits to either prompt take effect without a server restart.\n        system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n\n        # Track retry attempt for malformed JSON\n        retry_count = 0\n        raw_response = None\n\n        try:\n            response = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=2048,\n                temperature=0.3,  # Lower temperature for consistent classification\n            )\n\n            # Store raw response for error reporting\n            raw_response = response\n\n            # Strip markdown code fences if present (ZAI proxy wraps in ```json...```)\n            raw = response.strip()\n            if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n                raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n                raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n\n            # Parse JSON response\n            intents_data = json.loads(raw)\n            classifications = []\n\n            for intent_data in intents_data:\n                # Map string to IntentType enum\n                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n                try:\n                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n                except ValueError:\n                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n\n                classification = IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=intent_type,\n                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n                    lookup_kind=intent_data.get(\"lookup_kind\") if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP else None,\n                )\n                classifications.append(classification)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n            return classifications\n\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response as JSON: {e}\")\n\n            # Implement one corrective retry on malformed JSON\n            if retry_on_malformed and retry_count == 0:\n                logger.info(\"Malformed JSON detected, attempting corrective retry...\")\n                retry_count += 1\n                try:\n                    # Retry once with same parameters\n                    return await self.classify_utterance(\n                        utterance=utterance,\n                        session_id=session_id,\n                        retry_on_malformed=False,  # Prevent infinite retry\n                    )\n                except json.JSONDecode as retry_e:\n                    # Retry also failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n                    raise RouterMalformedError(\n                        parse_error=str(e),\n                        raw_output=raw_response or \"\",\n                        retry_count=retry_count,\n                    ) from retry_e\n\n            # No retry or retry failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n            raise RouterMalformedError(\n                parse_error=str(e),\n                raw_output=raw_response or \"\",\n                retry_count=retry_count,\n            ) from e\n\n        except (LLMTimeoutError, asyncio.TimeoutError) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\")\n            raise RouterTimeoutError(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\") from e\n\n        except (LLMRateLimitError,) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\")\n            raise RouterQuotaError(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\") from e\n\n        except (LLMError, httpx.HTTPError) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\")\n            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\") from e\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Classification failed with unexpected error: {e}\")\n            # Wrap unknown errors as RouterProxyError for graceful degradation\n            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM call failed: {e}\") from e\n\n    async def route_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        utterance_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n\n        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n            session_id: Session ID\n\n        Returns:\n            List of RoutedIntent objects\n\n        Raises:\n            RouterError: If router fails and broadcasts error event\n        \"\"\"\n        # Generate a fallback intent_id for error reporting\n        error_intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n        try:\n            # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n            # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n            # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n            classify_start = time.monotonic()\n            classifications = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n            router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n\n            routed_intents = []\n            for classification in classifications:\n                # Create intent ID\n                intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n                routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n                    intent_id=intent_id,\n                    classification=classification,\n                    session_id=session_id,\n                    utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n                    router_ms=router_ms,\n                )\n                routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n\n            return routed_intents\n\n        except RouterMalformedError as e:\n            # Malformed JSON after corrective retry - broadcast clarification card\n            logger.error(f\"Router malformed after retry: {e.parse_error}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_clarification_card(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                parse_error=e.parse_error,\n                retry_count=e.retry_count,\n                raw_output_snippet=e.raw_output[:200] if e.raw_output else None,\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n        except RouterTimeoutError as e:\n            # Router LLM timeout - broadcast router_unavailable\n            logger.error(f\"Router timeout: {e}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                error_reason=\"timeout\",\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n        except RouterQuotaError as e:\n            # Router quota exhausted - broadcast router_unavailable\n            logger.error(f\"Router quota exhausted: {e}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                error_reason=\"quota_exhausted\",\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n        except RouterProxyError as e:\n            # Router proxy unreachable - broadcast router_unavailable\n            logger.error(f\"Router proxy error: {e}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                error_reason=\"proxy_down\",\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n    async def process_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with status and data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n        # the dispatch itself.\n        timings = DispatchTimings()\n        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n\n        try:\n            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n            else:\n                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n        except Exception:\n            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n            raise\n\n        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n        return result\n\n    async def _persist_timings(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Fetch context then synthesize into structured result.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records fetch_first_source_ms,\n                fetch_total_ms, and synthesize_total_ms.\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with synthesized data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetching and synthesizing intent {routed_intent.intent_id} \"\n            f\"(type: {classification.intent_type.value})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Step 1: Fetch context — resolve project slug to local repo path\n            fetch_intent_type = self._map_intent_type(\n                classification.intent_type,\n                classification.lookup_kind\n            )\n\n            from ..environment.discovery import get_registry\n            from ..registry import get_project\n            repo_path = None\n            ssh_target = None\n            host_alias = None\n            registry = get_registry()\n            if registry and classification.project_slug:\n                entry = registry.lookup(classification.project_slug)\n                if entry:\n                    repo_path = str(entry.path)\n                    ssh_target = entry.ssh_target\n                    host_alias = entry.host\n                    location = entry.display_path\n                    logger.info(f\"Resolved '{classification.project_slug}' → {location}\")\n                else:\n                    logger.info(f\"No repo found for slug '{classification.project_slug}'\")\n\n            # YAML registry entry carries cluster/namespace/argocd_app — cluster\n            # drives ArgoCD endpoint resolution (bead adc-1ejh: the fetch strand\n            # resolves {argocd_api} from `cluster` via config/clusters.yaml).\n            # argocd_app defaults to the slug when omitted (see _fetch_argocd_app).\n            project_cfg = (\n                get_project(classification.project_slug)\n                if classification.project_slug else None\n            )\n\n            fetch_request = FetchRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                context=FetchContext(\n                    project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    repo_path=repo_path,\n                    ssh_target=ssh_target,\n                    host_alias=host_alias,\n                    cluster=project_cfg.get(\"cluster\") if project_cfg else None,\n                    namespace=project_cfg.get(\"namespace\") if project_cfg else None,\n                    app_name=project_cfg.get(\"argocd_app\") if project_cfg else None,\n                ),\n            )\n\n            # fetch_first_source_ms = time from fetch start to the first source\n            # resolving (success/fail/timeout — the first progress state on the\n            # pending card). fetch_total_ms = the fetch window close.\n            fetch_start = timings.clock()\n            first_source_at: list[float | None] = [None]\n\n            def _on_first_source(_source, _result) -> None:\n                if first_source_at[0] is None:\n                    first_source_at[0] = timings.clock()\n\n            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(fetch_request, _on_first_source)\n            timings.record(\"fetch_total_ms\", fetch_result.total_duration_ms)\n            if first_source_at[0] is not None:\n                timings.record(\n                    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n                    timings.elapsed_ms(fetch_start, first_source_at[0]),\n                )\n\n            # Check for terminal failure: ALL sources failed (Degraded-State UX)\n            if fetch_result.terminal_failure == \"all_sources_failed\":\n                logger.error(\n                    f\"All fetch sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: \"\n                    f\"{len(fetch_result.coverage.failed)} failed, \"\n                    f\"{len(fetch_result.coverage.timed_out)} timed out\"\n                )\n\n                # Build failed sources list for error event\n                failed_sources = []\n                for source, result in fetch_result.sources.items():\n                    failed_sources.append({\n                        \"source\": source.value,\n                        \"status\": result.status,\n                        \"error\": result.error,\n                    })\n\n                # Broadcast all_sources_failed error event\n                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n                await handler.broadcast_all_sources_failed(\n                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                    failed_sources=failed_sources,\n                )\n\n                # Update intent status to failed\n                store = get_store()\n                await store.update_intent_status(\n                    routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"failed\",\n                    \"All fetch sources failed\",\n                )\n\n                return {\n                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n                    \"error\": \"all_sources_failed\",\n                    \"message\": \"No data — all required sources failed\",\n                    \"terminal_failure\": \"all_sources_failed\",\n                }\n\n            # Check for required source failures (terminal condition)\n            required_sources_failed = [\n                s for s in fetch_result.coverage.failed\n                if any(cmd.source == s and cmd.required\n                       for cmd in get_fetch_commands(fetch_result.intent_type))\n            ]\n\n            if required_sources_failed:\n                failure_reason = f\"Required data sources failed: {', '.join(s.value for s in required_sources_failed)}\"\n                logger.error(f\"Required sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {failure_reason}\")\n\n                # Handle terminal failure\n                await self._handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n                    routed_intent=routed_intent,\n                    failure_reason=failure_reason,\n                    error_type=\"required_source_failure\",\n                )\n\n                return {\n                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n                    \"error\": failure_reason,\n                    \"message\": \"Required sources failed - cannot proceed\",\n                }\n\n            # Step 2: Synthesize result\n            synthesize_request = SynthesizeRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                fetched_context=fetch_result,\n                urgency=classification.urgency,\n            )\n\n            synth_start = timings.clock()\n            try:\n                synthesize_result = await synthesize_intent(synthesize_request)\n            except Exception as synth_e:\n                # ZAI failure at synthesize stage - broadcast degraded_raw_data\n                # (Degraded-State UX: fetched data is never discarded)\n                logger.error(\n                    f\"Synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {synth_e}\"\n                )\n\n                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n                await handler.broadcast_degraded_raw_data(\n                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                    fetched_context=fetch_result,\n                    error_reason=str(synth_e),\n                )\n\n                # Update intent status to failed\n                store = get_store()\n                await store.update_intent_status(\n                    routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"failed\",\n                    f\"Synthesize failed: {synth_e}\",\n                )\n\n                return {\n                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n                    \"error\": \"synthesize_failed\",\n                    \"message\": \"Summary unavailable — showing raw fetch data\",\n                    \"degraded\": True,\n                }\n\n            timings.record(\"synthesize_total_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(synth_start))\n            # synthesize_first_token_ms is not measurable on the current\n            # call_simple path (no token stream) and is left NULL until the\n            # synthesize strand streams — see src/instrument/timings.py.\n\n            # Persist result to session store so loadTopics() can display it\n            store = get_store()\n            _topic_type_map = {\n                IntentType.ACTION: \"project\",\n                IntentType.TASK_PROFILE: \"project\",\n            }\n            topic_type = _topic_type_map.get(classification.intent_type, \"research\")\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=classification.utterance_fragment or routed_intent.utterance[:80],\n                topic_type=topic_type,\n                project_slugs=[classification.project_slug] if classification.project_slug else [],\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n            await store.link_intent_to_topic(routed_intent.intent_id, topic_id)\n\n            # Derive result_type from intent classification (includes lookup_kind for lookups)\n            result_type = derive_result_type(\n                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                lookup_kind=classification.lookup_kind,\n            )\n\n            result_id = await store.create_result(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                summary=synthesize_result.summary,\n                data=synthesize_result.data,\n                urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n                result_type=result_type,\n            )\n\n            # Render card via hot-path selector (deterministic, no LLM)\n            renderer = get_renderer()\n            render_outcome = renderer.render(\n                result_id=result_id,\n                result_type=result_type,\n                result_data=synthesize_result.data,\n                summary=synthesize_result.summary,\n                urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n            )\n\n            # Update result's card_fallback flag so client knows which path to take\n            await store.update_result_card_fallback(result_id, render_outcome.card_fallback)\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"resolved\",\n                \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                \"result_id\": result_id,\n                \"data\": synthesize_result.data,\n                \"summary\": synthesize_result.summary,\n                \"urgency\": synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n                \"coverage\": synthesize_result.coverage,\n                \"caveats\": synthesize_result.caveats,\n                \"card_fallback\": render_outcome.card_fallback,\n                \"rendered_html\": render_outcome.rendered_html,\n                \"component_id\": render_outcome.component_id,\n                \"message\": \"Intent synthesized successfully\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Fetch/synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n            # Detect terminal failure and handle appropriately\n            await self._handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n                routed_intent=routed_intent,\n                failure_reason=str(e),\n                error_type=\"worker_crash\",\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"failed\",\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"message\": \"Fetch/synthesize failed - terminal error\",\n            }\n\n    async def _handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        failure_reason: str,\n        error_type: str,\n        bead_ref: str | None = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Handle terminal failure for a routed intent.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The intent that failed\n            failure_reason: Human-readable failure reason\n            error_type: Type of error (worker_crash, invalid_input, required_source_failure)\n            bead_ref: Associated bead reference (if applicable)\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..escalate.handler import handle_terminal_failure\n\n        # failure_reason=failure_reason,\n            error_type=error_type,\n            bead_ref=bead_ref,\n        )\n\n    def _map_intent_type(self, intent_type: IntentType, lookup_kind: str | None = None) -> FetchIntentType:\n        \"\"\"Map router IntentType to fetch IntentType.\n\n        For lookup intents, route to the subtype-specific fetch matrix based on lookup_kind.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Map enum values by string\n        type_map = {\n            IntentType.STATUS: FetchIntentType.STATUS,\n            IntentType.ACTION: FetchIntentType.ACTION,\n            IntentType.BRAINSTORM: FetchIntentType.BRAINSTORM,\n            IntentType.LOOKUP: FetchIntentType.LOOKUP,\n            IntentType.REMINDER: FetchIntentType.REMINDER,\n            IntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION: FetchIntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n            IntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG: FetchIntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG,\n            IntentType.STUCK: FetchIntentType.STUCK,\n        }\n\n        # For lookup intents with lookup_kind, route to the subtype-specific matrix\n        if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP and lookup_kind:\n            if lookup_kind == \"logs\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_LOGS\n            elif lookup_kind == \"config\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_CONFIG\n            elif lookup_kind == \"docs\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_DOCS\n\n        return type_map.get(intent_type, FetchIntentType.STATUS)\n\n    async def _check_fence_for_bead(\n        self,\n        bead_ref: str,\n    ) -> dict | None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if a bead has been fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at set).\n\n        Args:\n            bead_ref: The bead reference to check\n\n        Returns:\n            Fence context dict with bead_id, refusal_reason, refusal_count, fenced_at\n            if fenced, None otherwise.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n       last_refusal_reason = bead_watch.get(\"last_refusal_reason\")\n            fenced_at = bead_watch.get(\"fenced_at\")\n            refusal_count = bead_watch.get(\"refusal_count\", 0)\n\n            if last_refusal_reason or fenced_at:\n                # Bead is fenced - extract fence context\n                return {\n                    \"bead_id\": bead_ref,\n                    \"refusal_reason\": last_refusal_reason or \"Fenced (no reason provided)\",\n                    \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                    \"fenced_at\": fenced_at,\n                }\n\n            return None\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Error checking fence status for bead {bead_ref}: {e}\")\n            return None\n\n    async def _create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        fence_context: dict,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a stuck card when a fenced bead is detected during intent routing.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent\n            fence_context: Fence context from _check_fence_for_bead\n\n        Returns:\n f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n\n            # Update intent type to 'stuck' and status to 'stuck'\n            await               label=f\"Fenced: {routed_intent.utterance[:80]}\",\n                topic_type=topic_type,\n                project_slugs=[routed_intent.classification.project_slug] if routed_intent.classification.project_slug else [],\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n\n            # Link intent to topic\n            await store.link_intent_to_topic(routed_intent.intent_id, topic_id)\n\n            # Create stuck result\n            summary = f\"Task stuck — needs your input\"\n            data = {\n                \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                \"stuck_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                \"message\": f\"This task has been blocked after {refusal_count} refusals.\",\n                \"action_hint\": \"Review the bead and provide the missing information or context needed to proceed.\",\n                \"fence_detected_during\": \"intent_routing\",\n            }\n\n            # Derive result_type from original intent classification (not \"stuck\" state, includes lookup_kind)\n            result_type = derive_result_type(\n                intent_type=routed_intent.classification.intent_type.value,\n                project_slug=routed_intent.classification.project_slug,\n                lookup_kind=routed_intent.classification.lookup_kind,\n            )\n\n            result_id = await store.create_result(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                summary=summary,\n                data=data,\n                urgency=\"high\",\n                result_type=result_type,\n            )\n\n            # Render stuck card via hot-path selector (deterministic, no LLM)\n            renderer = get_renderer()\n            render_outcome = renderer.render(\n                result_id=result_id,\n                result_type=result_type,\n                result_data=data,\n                summary=summary,\n                urgency=\"high\",\n            )\n\n            # Update result's card_fallback flag\n            await store.update_result_card_fallback(result_id, render_outcome.card_fallback)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Created stuck card {result_id} for fenced bead {bead_id}\")\n\n            # Broadcast task_stuck event via SSE\n            broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n            await broadcaster.broadcast(\n                SSEEvent(\n                    event_type=EventType.TASK_STUCK,\n                    data={\n                        \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n \"session_id\": routed_intent.session_id,\n                        \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                        \"timestamp\": int(datetime.now().timestamp()),\n                    },\n                    target_session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                )\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": a NEEDLESS bead.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to escalate\n            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records escalate_ms.\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with pending card or stuck card if fence detected\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # Step 1: Check for existing fenced beads in the session before escalating\n        # This detects fence events when last_refusal_reason is set on existing beads\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            fenced_beads = self._create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n                    routed_intent, fence_context\n                )\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Error checking for fenced beads: {e}\")\n            # Continue to escalation if fence check fails\n\n        # Step 2: No fenced beads detected - proceed with escalation\n        # Build escalate request\n        escalate_request = EscalateRequest(\n            intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n            session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n            intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n            project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n            context={\n                \"reasoning\": classification.reasoning,\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n            },\n            metadata={\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n                \"confidence\": classification.confidence,\n            },\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Escalate to bead — escalate_ms budgets formulation + validation +\n            # bf create (task-profile dispatches only; NULL for hot-path rows).\n            esc_start = timings.clock()\n            result = await escalate_intent(escalate_request)\n            timings.record(\"escalate_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(esc_start))\n\n            # Check if the created bead is immediately fenced (race condition:\n            # watcher may have fenced it between escalate request and now)\n            if result.bead_id:\n                fence_context =           \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"escalated\",\n                \"bead_id\": result.bead_id,\n                \"pending_card\": {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n _evaluate_auto_approve(self, request: EscalateRequest, exceptions_config: dict) -> tuple[bool, str | None]:\n176\t        \"\"\"\n177\t        Evaluate if an intent should be auto-approved based on exceptions.yaml.\n178\t\n179\t        Args:\n180\t            request: The escalate request\n181\t            exceptions_config: The loaded exceptions.yaml config\n182\t\n183\t        Returns:\n184\t            (auto_approve: bool, reason: str | None)\n185\t        \"\"\"\n186\t        auto_approve_rules = exceptions_config.get(\"auto_approve\", {})\n187\t\n188\t        # Extract context for rule evaluation\n189\t        context = {\n190\t            \"environment\": request.metadata.get(\"environment\"),\n191\t            \"project_slug\": request.project_slug,\n192\t            \"branch\": request.metadata.get(\"branch\"),\n193\t            \"action\": request.metadata.get(\"action\"),\n194\t            \"risk_level\": request.metadata.get(\"risk_level\", \"normal\"),\n195\t        }\n196\t\n197\t        # Check read-only operations (always auto-approved)\n198\t        read_only_actions = auto_approve_rules.get(\"read_only\", [])\n199\t        action = context.get(\"action\")\n200\t        if action in read_only_actions:\n201\t            return True, f\"Read-only operation: {action}\"\n202\t\n203\t        # Check safe mutations with conditions\n204\t        safe_mutations = auto_approve_rules.get(\"safe_mutations\", [])\n205\t        for rule in safe_mutations:\n206\t            condition = rule.get(\"condition\", \"\")\n207\t            actions = rule.get(\"actions\", [])\n208\t\n209\t            # Simple condition evaluation (supports basic comparisons)\n210\t            if self._evaluate_condition(condition, context) and action in actions:\n211\t                return True, f\"Safe mutation in {condition}: {action}\"\n212\t\n213\t        # Check manual approval rules (never auto-approve)\n214\t        manual_rules = exceptions_config.get(\"manual_approval\", [])\n215\t        for rule in manual_rules:\n216\t            condition = rule.get(\"condition\", \"\")\n217\t            actions = rule.get(\"actions\", [])\n218\t\n219\t            # Check if the action is in this rule's actions\n220\t            if action not in actions:\n221\t                continue\n222\t\n223\t            # Evaluate the condition\n224\t            if self._evaluate_condition(condition, context):\n225\t                if rule.get(\"always_approve\") is False:\n226\t                    return False, f\"Manual approval required: {condition}\"\n227\t\n228\t        # Check approval workflow rules\n229\t        approval_config = exceptions_config.get(\"approval\", {})\n230\t        never_auto_approve = approval_config.get(\"never_auto_approve\", [])\n231\t        for condition_str in never_auto_approve:\n232\t            if self._evaluate_condition(condition_str, context):\n233\t                return False, f\"Never auto-approve: {condition_str}\"\n234\t\n235\t        # Default: require manual approval for unknown actions\n236\t        return False, \"Unknown action - requires manual approval\"\n237\t\n238\t    def _evaluate_condition(self, condition: str, context: dict) -> bool:\n239\t        \"\"\"\n240\t        Evaluate a condition string against context.\n241\t\n242\t        Supports simple conditions like:\n243\t        - \"environment == 'staging'\"\n244\t        - \"branch == 'main' || branch == 'master'\"  (using || for OR)\n245\t        - \"action == 'kubectl_delete_namespace'\"\n246\t        - \"environment == 'staging' && action == 'restart'\"  (using && for AND)\n247\t\n248\t        Note: Supports both shell-style operators (||, &&) and Python-style (or, and).\n249\t\n250\t        Args:\n251\t            condition: The condition string\n252\t            context: Dict of available variables\n253\t\n254\t        Returns:\n255\t            True if condition evaluates to True, False otherwise\n256\t        \"\"\"\n257\t        try:\n258\t            # Translate shell-style operators to Python syntax\n259\t            # (People are more familiar with || and && from bash/js)\n260\t            python_condition = condition.replace(\" || \", \" or \").replace(\" && \", \" and \")\n261\t\n262\t            # Create a safe evaluation environment\n263\t            safe_globals = {\"__builtins__\": {}}\n264\t            safe_locals = {\n265\t                \"environment\": context.get(\"environment\"),\n266\t                \"project_slug\": context.get(\"project_slug\"),\n267\t                \"branch\": context.get(\"branch\"),\n268\t                \"action\": context.get(\"action\"),\n269\t                \"risk_level\": context.get(\"risk_level\"),\n270\t            }\n271\t\n272\t            # Evaluate condition\n273\t            result = eval(python_condition, safe_globals, safe_locals)\n274\t            return bool(result)\n275\t        except Exception as e:\n276\t            logger.warning(f\"Failed to evaluate condition '{condition}': {e}\")\n277\t            return False\n278\t\n279\t    def _get_bead_type_from_targets(self, intent_type: str, exceptions_config: dict) -> str:\n280\t        \"\"\"\n281\t        Get the bead type from escalation_targets config.\n282\t\n283\t        Args:\n284\t            intent_type: The intent type from IntentRouter (e.g., 'action', 'task-profile', 'self-modification')\n285\t            exceptions_config: The loaded exceptions.yaml target_config = escalation_targets.get(escalation_key, {})\n303\t        return target_config.get(\"bead_type\", \"task\")\n304\t\n305\t    async def _execute_auto_approved(self, request: request: EscalateRequest,\n439\t    ) -> str:\n440\t        \"\"\"\n441\t        Formulate a bead body using the LLM.\n442\t\n443\t        Takes the intent and context, produces a well-structured bead body.\n444\t        \"\"\"\n445\t        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n446\t        system_prompt = load_escalate_prompt()\n447\t\n448\t        # Build context for the LLM\n449\t        context_parts = [\n450\t            f\"User utterance: {request.utterance}\",\n451\t            f\"Intent type: {request.intent_type}\",\n452\t        ]\n453\t\n454\t        if request.project_slug:\n455\t            context_parts.append(f\"Project: {request.project_slug}\")\n456\t\n457\t        if request.topic_id:\n458\t            context_parts.append(f\"Topic ID: {request.topic_id}\")\n459\t\n460\t        # Add any additional context\n461\t        if request.context:\n462\t            context_parts.append(f\"\\nAdditional context:\")\n463\t            for key, value in request.context.items():\n464\t                context_parts.append(f\"  {key}: {value}\")\n465\t\n466\t        # Build the user message\n467\t        user_message = \"\\n\".join([\n468\t            \"Formulate a NEEDLE bead body for this intent:\",\n469\t            \"\",\n470\t            *context_parts,\n471\t            \"\",\n472\t            \"The bead body should be clear, actionable, and structured for Claude Code execution.\",\n473\t        ])\n474\t\n475\t        logger.info(f\"Formulating bead body for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n476\t\n477\t        # Make LLM call\n478\t        try:\n479\t            bead_body = await client.call_simple(\n480\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n481\t                user_message=user_message,\n482\t                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n483\t                max_tokens=4096,\n484\t                temperature=0.7,\n485\t            )\n486\t\n487\t            logger.info(f\"Formulated bead body for intent {request.intent_id}: {len(bead_body)} chars\")\n488\t            return bead_body\n489\t\n490\t        except Exception as e:\n491\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\")\n492\t            raise EscalateError(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\") from e\n493\t\n494\t    async def create_bead(\n495\t        self,\n496\t        request: EscalateRequest,\n497\t        bead_body: str,\n498\t    ) -> str:\n499\t        \"\"\"\n500\t        Create a bead using the bf CLI.\n501\t\n502\t        Returns the bead ID.\n503\t        \"\"\"\n504\t        # Generate bead title from intent\n505\t        title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n506\t\n507\t        # Prepare metadata (includes session_id for bead watcher routing)\n508\t        metadata = {\n509\t            \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n510\t            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n511\t            \"intent_type\": request.intent_type,\n512\t            \"origin_surface_id\": request.metadata.get(\"surface_id\"),\n513\t            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n514\t        }\n515\t\n516\t        if request.project_slug:\n517\t            metadata[\"project_slug\"] = request.project_slug\n518\t\n519\t        if request.topic_id:\n520\t            metadata[\"topic_id\"] = request.topic_id\n521\t\n522\t        # Build bf create command\n523\t        # Using bf CLI to create the bead\n524\t        # Note: bf uses --description for the main body content\n525\t        try:\n526\t            # Build command with description (escape bead body for shell)\n527\t            cmd = [\n528\t                \"bf\",\n529\t                \"create\",\n530\t                \"--title\", title,\n531\t                \"--type\", \"task\",  # task-profile intents create task beads\n532\t                \"--description\", bead_body,\n533\t            ]\n534\t\n535\t            # Add labels for metadata tracking\n536\t            # Labels are simple strings, we'll encode key=value for structured data\n537\t            for key, value in metadata.items():\n538\t                if value is not None:\n539\t                    cmd.extend([\"--label\", f\"{key}={value}\"])\n540\t\n541\t            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n542\t                *cmd,\n543\t                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n544\t                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n545\t            )\n546\t\n547\t            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n548\t\n549\t            if proc.returncode != 0:\n550\t                error_msg = stderr.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n551\t                logger.error(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n552\t                raise BeadCreationError(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n553\t\n554\t            # Parse bead ID from output\n555\t            output = stdout.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n556\t            bead_id = self._extract_bead_id(output)\n557\t\n558\t            logger.info(f\"Created bead {bead_id} for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n559\t            return bead_id\n560\t\n561\t        except FileNotFoundError:\n562\t            logger.error(\"bf CLI not found\")\n563\t            raise BeadCreationError(\"bf CLI not found\")\n564\t        except Exception as e:\n565\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\")\n566\t            raise BeadCreationError(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\") from e\n567\t\n568\t    async def _create_bead_with_type(\n569\t        self,\n570\t        request: EscalateRequest,\n571\t        bead_body: str,\n572\t        bead_type: str,\n573\t    ) -> str:\n574\t        \"\"\"\n575\t        Create a bead with a specific type using the bf CLI.\n576\t\n577\t        Returns the bead ID.\n578\t        \"\"\"\n579\t        # Generate bead title from intent\n580\t        title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n581\t\n582\t int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n589\t        }\n590\t\n591\t        if request.project_slug:\n592\t            metadata[\"project_slug\"] = request.project_slug\n593\t\n594\t        if \"bf\",\n601\t                \"create\",\n602\t                \"--title\", title,\n603\t                \"--type\", bead_type,\n604\t                \"--description\", bead_body,\n605\t            ]\n606\t\n607\t            # Add labels for metadata tracking\n608\t            for key, value in metadata.items():\n609\t                if value is not None:\n610\t                    cmd.extend([\"--label\", f\"{key}={value}\"])\n611\t\n612\t            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n613\t                *cmd,\n614\t                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n615\t                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n616\t            )\n617\t\n618\t            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n619\t\n620\t            if proc.returncode != 0:\n621\t                error_msg = stderr.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n622\t                logger.error(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n623\t                raise BeadCreationError(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n624\t\n625\t            # Parse bead ID from output\n626\t            output = stdout.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n627\t            bead_id = self._extract_bead_id(output)\n628\t\n629\t            logger.info(f\"Created bead {bead_id} (type: {bead_type}) for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n630\t            return bead_id\n631\t\n632\t        except FileNotFoundError:\n633\t            logger.error(\"bf CLI not found\")\n634\t            raise BeadCreationError(\"bf CLI not found\")\n635\t        except Exception as e:\n636\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\")\n637\t            raise BeadCreationError(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\") from e\n638\t\n639\t    async def _create_bead_watch(\n640\t        self,\n641\t        bead_ref: str,\n642\t        project_slug: str | None,\n643\t        intent_type: str,\n644\t    ) -> None:\n645\t        \"\"\"Create bead_watch row for circuit breaker tracking.\n646\t\n647\t        Plan §10 The Async Path: watcher tracks open beads for refusals/SLA.\n648\t        SLA defaults by intent_type, with per-project sla_hours override.\n649\t\n650\t        Args:\n651\t            bead_ref: The bead ID to watch\n652\t            project_slug: Project slug for SLA override lookup\n653\t            intent_type: Intent type for default SLA lookup\n654\t        \"\"\"\n655\t        from ..registry import get_project\n656\t\n657\t        sla_hours = None\n658\t\n659\t        # Check for per-project SLA override\n660\t is not None:\n665\t                    logger.info(\n666\t                        f\"Using per-project SLA override for {project_slug}: \"\n667\t                        f\"{sla_hours} hours\"\n668\t                    )\n669\t\n670\t        store = await self._get_store()\n671\t        await store.create_bead_watch(\n672\t            bead_ref=bead_ref,\n673\t            sla_hours=sla_hours,\n674\t            intent_type=intent_type,\n675\t        )\n676\t        logger.debug(f\"Created bead_watch row for {bead_ref}\")\n677\t\n678\t    def _generate_bead_title(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> str:\n679\t        \"\"\"Generate a bead title from the request.\"\"\"\n680\t        # Use utterance prefix as title\n681\t        utterance = request.utterance.strip()\n682\t        if len(utterance) > 60:\n683\t            title = utterance[:57] + \"...\"\n684\t        else:\n685\t            title = utterance\n686\t\n687\t        # Add project prefix if available\n688\t        if request.project_slug:\n689\t            title = f\"[{request.project_slug}] {title}\"\n690\t\n691\t        return title\n692\t\n693\t    def _extract_bead_id(self, output: str) -> str:\n694\t        \"\"\"Extract bead ID from bf create output.\n695\t\n696\t        bf create outputs just the bead ID (e.g., \"abc-123\").\n697\t        May include prefix text like \"Created bead abc-123\" in some versions.\n698\t        \"\"\"\n699\t        import re\n700\t\n701\t        # Strip whitespace first\n702\t        output = output.strip()\n703\t\n704\t        if not output:\n705\t            logger.warning(f\"Empty bf create output\")\n706\t            return str(uuid.uuid4())\n707\t\n708\t        # Try to extract bead ID pattern: lowercase letters, dash, alphanumeric\n709\t        # Bead IDs typically include numbers like: abc-123, xyz-789, adc-2fs\n710\t        # We prioritize matches with digits over letter-only matches\n711\t        patterns = [\n712\t            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]*[0-9]+[a-z0-9]*\",  # Three components with digit: test-bead-456\n713\t            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]*[0-9]+[a-z0-9]*\",          # Two components with digit: abc-123\n714\t            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]+\",                  # Three components letter-only: test-bead-xyz\n715\t            r\"[a-z]{3,}-[a-z0-9]{2,}\",                   # Fallback: any 3+ char prefix, dash, 2+ char suffix\n716\t        ]\n717\t\n718\t        for pattern in patterns:\n719\t            matches = re.findall(pattern, output)\n720\t            if matches:\n721\t                # Return the longest match (prefer more specific patterns)\n722\t                bead_id = max(matches, key=len)\n723\t                logger.debug(f\"Extracted bead ID '{bead_id}' from output\")\n724\t                return bead_id\n725\t\n726\t        # Fallback: if output already looks like a bead ID, return as-is\n727\t        # Basic validation: should contain a dash and be reasonable length\n728\t        if \"-\" in output and len(output) >= 3:\n729\t            logger.warning(f\"Using output as bead ID despite non-standard format: {output}\")\n730\t            return output\n731\t\n732\t        # Last resort: return the whole output (bf might have changed format)\n733\t        logger.warning(f\"Unexpected bf create output format: {output}\")\n734\t        return output\n735\t\n736\t    async def _validate_and_prepare_approval(\n737\t        self,\n738\t        request: EscalateRequest,\n739\t        bead_body: str,\n740\t        bead_type: str,\n741\t    ) -> tuple[ValidationResult, str | None]:\n742\t        \"\"\"\n743\t        Validate bead body and prepare for approval if needed.\n744\t\n745\t        Args:\n746\t            request: The escalate request\n747\t            bead_body: The formulated bead body\n748\t            bead_type: The bead type\n749\t\n750\t        Returns:\n751\t            (validation_result, reformulated_bead_body_or_None)\n752\t\n753\t        Raises:\n754\t            BeadApprovalRequired: When bead requires approval (carries approval card)\n755\t            ValidationRetryExhaustedError: When validation fails after re-formulation\n756\t        \"\"\"\n757\t        validator = get_validator()\n758\t\n759\t        # Initial validation\n760\t        result = validator.validate_bead_body(bead_body, bead_type)\n761\t\n762\t        if result.is_valid and not result.requires_approval:\n763\t            logger.info(f\"Validation passed for intent {request.intent_id} (no approval required)\")\n764\t            return result, None\n765\t\n766\t        if result.requires_approval:\n767\t            logger.info(f\"Validation passed but approval required for intent {request.intent_id}: {result.approval_requirement.reason}\")\n768\t            # Build approval card\n769\t            approval_card = self._build_approval_card(\n770\t                request=request,\n771\t                bead_body=bead_body,\n772\t                bead_type=bead_type,\n773\t                validation_result=result,\n774\t            )\n775\t            raise BeadApprovalRequired(approval_card, bead_body, bead_type)\n776\t\n777\t        # Validation failed - check re-formulation count before attempting\n778\t        store = await self._get_store()\n779\t        reformulation_count = await store.get_reformulation_count(request.session_id)\n780\t        MAX_REFORMULATION_ATTEMPTS = 3  # Maximum 3 attempts per session\n781\t\n782\t        if reformulation_count >= MAX_REFORMULATION_ATTEMPTS:\n783\t            logger.error(\n784\t                f\"Session {request.session_id} has exceeded re-formulation attempt limit \"\n785\t                f\"({reformulation_count}/{MAX_REFORMULATION_ATTEMPTS})\"\n786\t            )\n787\t            raise ValidationRetryExhaustedError(\n788\t                result.violations,\n789\t                f\"Re-formulation attempt limit exceeded ({MAX_REFORMULATION_ATTEMPTS} attempts per session). \"\n790\t                \"Please clarify your request or provide more specific details.\",\n791\t            )\n792\t\n793\t        # Increment re-formulation count\n794\t        new_count = await store.increment_reformulation_count(request.session_id)\n795\t        logger.info(f\"Attempt re-formulation for intent {request.intent_id} (session attempt {new_count}/{MAX_REFORMULATION_ATTEMPTS})\")\n796\t        logger.warning(f\"Validation failed for intent {request.intent_id}, attempting re-formulation\")\n797\t        logger.info(f\"Reformulation hint: {result.reformulation_hint}\")\n798\t\n799\t        try:\n800\t            # Re-formulate with failure reason\n801\t            reformulated_body = await self._reformulate_with_failure_reason(\n802\t                request=request,\n803\t                original_body=bead_body,\n804\t                validation_result=result,\n805\t            )\n806\t\n807\t            # Validate the reformulated body\n808\t            new_result = validator.validate_bead_body(reformulated_body, bead_type)\n809\t\n810\t            if not new_result.is_valid:\n811\t                # Re-formulation still failed\n812\t                logger.error(f\"Re-formulation still failed for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n813\t                raise ValidationRetryExhaustedError(\n814\t                    new_result.violations,\n815\t                    f\"Validation failed after re-formulation: {len(new_result.violations)} violations remain\",\n816\t                )\n817\t\n818\t            if new_result.requires_approval:\n819\t                logger.info(f\"Re-formulated body requires approval\")\n820\t                approval_card = self._build_approval_card(\n821\t                    request=request,\n822\t                    bead_body=reformulated_body,\n823\t                    bead_type=bead_type,\n824\t                    validation_result=new_result,\n825\t                )\n826\t                raise BeadApprovalRequired(approval_card, reformulated_body, bead_type)\n827\t\n828\t            logger.info(f\"Re-formulated body passed validation\")\n829\t            return new_result, reformulated_body\n830\t\n831\t        except Exception as e:\n832\t            logger.error(f\"Re-formulation failed for intent {request.intent_id}: {e}\")\n833\t            raise ValidationRetryExhaustedError(\n834\t                result.violations,\n835\t                f\"Re-formulation failed: {str(e)}\",\n836\t            )\n837\t\n838\t    async def _reformulate_with_failure_reason(\n839\t        self,\n840\t        request: EscalateRequest,\n841\t        original_body: str,\n842\t        validation_result: ValidationResult,\n843\t    ) -> str:\n844\t        \"\"\"\n845\t        Re-formulate bead body with validation failure reason.\n846\t\n847\t        Args:\n848\t            request: The escalate request\n849\t            original_body: The original bead body\n850\t            validation_result: The validation result with violations\n851\t\n852\t        Returns:\n853\t            Reformulated bead body\n854\t        \"\"\"\n855\t        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n856\t        system_prompt = load_escalate_prompt()\n857\t\n858\t        # Build failure message\n859\t        failure_message = \"The previous bead body failed validation:\\n\\n\"\n860\t        for violation in validation_result.violations:\n861\t            failure_message += f\"- {violation.message}\\n\"\n862\t        if validation_result.reformulation_hint:\n863\t            failure_message += f\"\\nHint: {validation_result.reformulation_hint}\"\n864\t\n865\t        # Build user message\n866\t        user_message = f\"\"\"Original intent: {request.utterance}\n867\t\n868\t{failure_message}\n869\t\n870\tPlease re-formulate the bead body to address these validation errors.\n871\tOutput ONLY the revised bead body as markdown.\n872\t\"\"\"\n873\t\n874\t        logger.info(f\"Requesting re-formulation for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n875\t\n876\t        try:\n877\t            reformulated = await client.call_simple(\n878\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n879\t                user_message=user_message,\n880\t                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n881\t                max_tokens=4096,\n882\t                temperature=0.7,\n883\t            )\n884\t\n885\t            logger.info(f\"Re-formulated bead body for intent {request.intent_id}: {len(reformulated)} chars\")\n886\t            return reformulated\n887\t\n888\t        except Exception as e:\n889\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to re-formulate bead body: {e}\")\n890\t self,\n928\t        request: EscalateRequest,\n929\t        bead_type: str,\n930\t        validation_result: dict,\n931\t    ) -> str:\n932\t        \"\"\"\n933\t        Build an approval narration      title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n1004\t\n1005\t        # Format violations for display\n1006\t        violation_messages = []\n1007\t        for v in violations:\n1008\t },\n1027\t        }\n1028\t\n1029\t    async def escalate_intent(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> EscalateResult:\n1030\t        \"\"\"\n1031\t        Escalate an intent to a bead.\n1032\t\n1033\t        Full flow:\n1034\t        1. Load exceptions.yaml to determine bead type from escalation_targets\n1035\t        2. Evaluate auto-approve rules from exceptions.yaml\n1036\t        3. If auto-approved: execute directly and return result\n1037\t        4. Otherwise: formulate bead body via LLM, validate, create bead (or approval card), return result\n1038\t\n1039\t        Args:\n1040\t            request: The escalate request\n1041\t\n1042\t        Returns:\n1043\t            EscalateResult with bead ID and pending card (if bead created)\n1044\t            or approval card (if approval required)\n1045\t            or execution result (if auto-approved)\n1046\t        \"\"\"\n1047\t        logger.info(f\"Escalating intent {request.intent_id}\")\n1048\t\n1049\t        try:\n1050\t            # Step 1: Load exceptions.yaml\n1051\t            reload_mgr = self._get_reload_manager()\n1052\t            exceptions_config = reload_mgr.get_config(\"exceptions\")\n1053\t\n1054\t            # Step 2: Evaluate auto-approve rules\n1055\t            auto_approve, reason = self._evaluate_auto_approve(request, exceptions_config)\n1056\t\n1057\t            if auto_approve:\n1058\t                logger.info(f\"Auto-approved intent {request.intent_id}: {reason}\")\n1059\t\n1060\t                # Execute directly without bead creation\n1061\t                execution_result = await self._execute_auto_approved(request)\n1062\t\n1063\t                # Update intent in store\n1064\t                store = await self._get_store()\n1065\t                await store.update_intent_status(\n1066\t                    intent_id=request.intent_id,\n1067\t                    status=\"resolved\",\n1068\t                )\n1069\t\n1070\t                # Return result with empty pending_card (auto-approved, no bead created)\n1071\t                return EscalateResult(\n1072\t                    bead_id=\"\",  # No bead created\n1073\t                    intent_id=request.intent_id,\n1074\t                    pending_card=execution_result,  # Return execution result directly\n1075\t                    status=\"completed\",\n1076\t                )\n1077\t\n1078\t            # Step 3: Determine bead type from escalation_targets\n1079\t            bead_type = self._get_bead_type_from_targets(request.intent_type, exceptions_config)\n1080\t            logger.info(f\"Creating bead type '{bead_type}' for intent {request.intent_id} (not auto-approved: {reason})\")\n1081\t\n1082\t            # Step 4: Formulate bead body\n1083\t            bead_body = await self.formulate_bead_body(request)\n1084\t\n1085\t            # Step 4.5: Validate and prepare for approval (NEW)\n1086\t            validation_result, reformulated_body = await self._validate_and_prepare_approval(\n1087\t                request=request,\n1088\t                bead_body=bead_body,\n1089\t                bead_type=bead_type,\n1090\t            )\n1091\t\n1092\t            # Use reformulated body if provided, otherwise original\n1093\t            final_body = reformulated_body or bead_body\n1094\t\n1095\t            # Step 5: Create bead with determined type\n1096\t            bead_id = await self._create_bead_with_type(request, final_body, bead_type)\n1097\t\n1098\t            # Step 5.5: Create bead_watch row for circuit breaker tracking\n1099\t            # (plan §10 The Async Path: watcher tracks open beads for refusals/SLA)\n1100\t            await self._create_bead_watch(\n1101\t                bead_ref=bead_id,\n1102\t                project_slug=request.project_slug,\n1103\t                intent_type=request.intent_type,\n1104\t            )\n1105\t\n1106\t            # Reset re-formulation count on successful bead creation\n1107\t            store = await self._get_store()\n1108\t            await store.reset_reformulation_count(request.session_id)\n1109\t            logger.info(f\"Reset reformulation_count for session {request.session_id} after successful bead creation\")\n1110\t\n1111\t            # Step 6: Build pending card\n1112\t            pending_card = self.build_pending_card(request, bead_id, bead_type)\n1113\t\n1114\t            # Update intent in store with bead reference\n1115\t            await store.update_intent_status(\n1116\t                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n1117\t                status=\"dispatched\",\n1118\t            )\n1119\t\n1120\t            logger.info(f\"Escalated intent {request.intent_id} to bead {bead_id}\")\n1121\t\n1122\t            return EscalateResult(\n1123\t                bead_id=bead_id,\n1124\t                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n1125\t                pending_card=pending_card,\n1126\t                status=\"created\",\n1127\t            )\n1128\t\n1129\t        except BeadApprovalRequired as e:\n1130\t            # Approval required - store pending approval and broadcast approval event\n1131\t            logger.info(f\"Approval required for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n1132\t\n1133\t            # Update intent status\n1134\t            store = await self._get_store()\n1135\t            await store.update_intent_status(\n1136\t                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n1137\t                status=\"awaiting_approval\",\n1138\t            )\n1139\t\n1140\t            # Store pending approval in database\n1141\t            approval_id = await store.create_pending_approval(\n1142\t                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n1143\t                session_id=request.session_id,\n1144\t                bead_body=e.bead_body,\n1145\t                bead_type=e.bead_type,\n1146\t
